methodology: Derived from customer KPI pyramids, 1-year product strategy horizons, and delivery activity using an OKR-inspired structure.

goals.title: 2024-Q1 Establish the first repeatable employer rollout model
goals.objective: Create a credible company-bike launch motion that employers can implement without heavy bespoke project work.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Time from contract signature to employee enrollment readiness.
Share of invited employees who complete enrollment and order.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Count of contracted employers operating active bike programs.

keyResults.target: Move from bespoke launches toward a reusable operating template
keyResults.baseline: Early employer launches require heavy manual coordination
keyResults.statement: Reduce program go-live lead time by standardizing policy setup, approvals, and employer onboarding tasks.

keyResults.target: Ensure launch waves create visible employee uptake
keyResults.baseline: Early rollouts depend strongly on employer communications quality
keyResults.statement: Increase enrollment conversion rate by making the value proposition and enrollment path simpler for employees.

keyResults.target: Grow the active employer base with launches that stick
keyResults.baseline: The portfolio is still proving its repeatability market by market
keyResults.statement: Increase employers live on platform by converting signed employers into successful live programs rather than stalled implementations.

goals.title: 2024-Q2 Remove the biggest rider conversion blockers
goals.objective: Make the first rider journey understandable enough that employees who are curious about the benefit actually complete an order.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of riders who convert from configured quote to confirmed order.
Elapsed time between first visit and completed order.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Count of riders with a live contract and recent activity.

keyResults.target: Convert more interested employees into confirmed bike orders
keyResults.baseline: Many riders hesitate when cost and process are not clear
keyResults.statement: Increase quote-to-order conversion by clarifying savings, contract steps, and dealer handover expectations.

keyResults.target: Shorten the path from exploration to commitment
keyResults.baseline: Order completion can stall across approval and partner handoffs
keyResults.statement: Reduce time to first confirmed order by removing unnecessary steps and improving order-status transparency.

keyResults.target: Build a meaningful active rider base from the first major launch cohorts
keyResults.baseline: Active-rider count still depends on successful first-order conversion
keyResults.statement: Increase active riders by improving first-order completion and early confidence after delivery.

goals.title: 2024-Q3 Make dealer execution predictable at higher leasing volume
goals.objective: Reduce operational rework for dealers so the platform can scale order volume without creating margin-destructive chaos in handover and service workflows.

linkedCustomers.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of orders received without missing information or manual repair work.

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Average time to close service cases.

linkedBusinessKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Coverage quality of active dealers and service partners across target markets.

keyResults.target: Deliver cleaner order packets into dealer workflows
keyResults.baseline: Manual corrections and missing data still create handover delays
keyResults.statement: Increase order completeness rate by validating order data before dealers accept fulfillment work.

keyResults.target: Reduce how long riders wait for repairs and authorized service work
keyResults.baseline: Turnaround varies widely by workshop and claim type
keyResults.statement: Reduce service turnaround time by standardizing intake, approvals, and reimbursement logic for partner workshops.

keyResults.target: Strengthen dealer and service footprint in priority regions
keyResults.baseline: Coverage quality remains uneven across cities and markets
keyResults.statement: Increase active partner coverage by improving onboarding, scorecards, and operational trust for dealers.

goals.title: 2024-Q4 Prove pooled bikes can work as an operational fleet
goals.objective: Show that shared bike fleets can be kept available, used regularly, and managed with enough operational discipline to justify expansion into fleet-style use cases.

linkedCustomers.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of fleet assets ready for operational use.

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Average weekly activity per pooled or assigned bike asset.

linkedBusinessKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Revenue from pooled bikes, assigned fleet contracts, and associated services.

keyResults.target: Keep pilot fleet assets ready for daily operational work
keyResults.baseline: Pilot readiness is vulnerable to maintenance gaps and unclear ownership
keyResults.statement: Increase asset availability by putting maintenance planning and asset-state controls into the pooled fleet workflow.

keyResults.target: Demonstrate that bikes are actually being used for real operational trips
keyResults.baseline: Utilization is still proving out in early pilots
keyResults.statement: Increase rides per asset per week by making booking, assignment, and issue reporting simple for operational users.

keyResults.target: Create evidence that fleet mobility can become a second product line
keyResults.baseline: Fleet revenue is emerging from early pilots rather than scaled contracts
keyResults.statement: Increase recurring revenue from fleet programs by converting initial pilots into multi-site subscriptions.

goals.title: 2025-Q1 Move from pilot-style launches to controlled scale
goals.objective: Scale the employer motion with more confidence by reducing setup effort, improving early employee uptake, and making program performance visible to HR and finance stakeholders.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of eligible employees who activate and keep using a company bike program.
Share of invited employees who complete enrollment and order.

linkedBusinessKPIs.kpiName: Net revenue retention from employer programs
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Revenue retention and expansion across employer accounts after churn, contraction, and expansion.

keyResults.target: Improve real usage, not just enrollment-ready access
keyResults.baseline: Adoption is still uneven after launch across employers and countries
keyResults.statement: Increase eligible employee bike adoption by improving launch quality, bike choice clarity, and service confidence early in the contract.

keyResults.target: Convert a higher share of invited employees into confirmed riders
keyResults.baseline: Conversion continues to drop when payroll and value communication are weak
keyResults.statement: Increase enrollment conversion rate through simpler onboarding and more transparent financial explanation.

keyResults.metric: Net revenue retention from employer programs
keyResults.target: Show that existing employer accounts can grow rather than churn
keyResults.baseline: Retention and expansion still depend on launch quality and support performance
keyResults.statement: Increase net revenue retention from employer programs by improving adoption and reducing operational reasons for employer dissatisfaction.

goals.title: 2025-Q2 Improve post-delivery rider confidence
goals.objective: Strengthen the rider experience after handover so employees keep commuting, recover faster from issues, and see the program as dependable rather than bureaucratic.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Average number of days per month a rider uses the bike for commuting or regular utility travel.
Average time to restore rider mobility after a reported issue.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of contracts that renew, upgrade, or convert into a next-term bike.

keyResults.target: Increase steady usage after the first delivery wave
keyResults.baseline: Some riders stop using the bike regularly after early friction
keyResults.statement: Increase active commuting days per rider by making service, support, and everyday ownership simpler after handover.

keyResults.target: Reduce rider downtime after service and claims events
keyResults.baseline: Recovery remains slow when partner coordination is unclear
keyResults.statement: Reduce incident recovery time through better case routing, status transparency, and partner turnaround management.

keyResults.target: Start turning first-contract riders into repeat lifecycle customers
keyResults.baseline: Renewal performance is still limited by weak lifecycle visibility
keyResults.statement: Increase renewal and upgrade rate by making contract, service history, and next-bike options easier to navigate.

goals.title: 2025-Q3 Build a healthier service-partner ecosystem
goals.objective: Improve partner economics and confidence so dealers stay engaged as volume grows and service work becomes a larger share of the value chain.

linkedCustomers.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedCustomerKPIs.kpiName: Dealer gross margin per completed bike order
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Dealer margin after discounts, handover effort, and rework for completed leasing orders.

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Time to reimburse or settle approved service and claims work.

linkedBusinessKPIs.customerName: Multi-location Dealer and Service Network Operator
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Share of dealer and service partners that leave or become inactive.

keyResults.metric: Dealer gross margin per completed bike order
keyResults.target: Make platform business economically worthwhile for dealers
keyResults.baseline: Margin is pressured by rework, delays, and unclear reimbursement
keyResults.statement: Increase dealer gross margin per completed bike order by improving order quality and reducing avoidable service friction.

keyResults.target: Pay partners faster for approved service and claims work
keyResults.baseline: Settlement lag continues to erode partner trust
keyResults.statement: Reduce reimbursement cycle time by tightening approval logic and finance handoffs for service cases.

keyResults.target: Stabilize the partner network in strategic regions
keyResults.baseline: Partner churn increases when platform economics are unclear
keyResults.statement: Reduce partner churn rate by making demand quality, reimbursement, and operating expectations more predictable.

goals.title: 2025-Q4 Prepare fleet mobility as a second growth engine
goals.objective: Turn pooled-bike pilots into a more credible product line by showing strong availability, repeat usage, and a cleaner expansion story for operators.

linkedCustomers.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of fleet assets ready for operational use.

linkedCustomerKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of eligible short-distance operational tasks completed using bikes instead of cars or vans.

linkedBusinessKPIs.customerName: Corporate Fleet and Field Operations Manager
linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Number of active campuses, depots, or operational sites using the fleet product.

keyResults.target: Keep fleet assets ready across pilot and expansion sites
keyResults.baseline: Operational reliability is still the main proof point for fleets
keyResults.statement: Increase asset availability by improving maintenance planning, asset monitoring, and ownership of fleet exceptions.

keyResults.target: Show that bikes are replacing real operational trips
keyResults.baseline: Trip substitution is not yet proven across all use cases
keyResults.statement: Increase fleet task completion with bikes by making booking and assignment fit day-to-day operational work.

keyResults.target: Expand from pilots into repeatable multi-site adoption
keyResults.baseline: Fleet growth is still constrained to early deployments
keyResults.statement: Increase fleet locations live by converting validated pilots into a standard offer for campuses and field operations.

description: Archived quarterly goals covering the past two years before the current quarter.
